title: Death and survival during the 1994 genocide in Rwanda
pages_or_sections: pp. 233-245; especially pp. 236-245, Tables 2-5, Appendix B
strengths: Registry-style Ibuka victim file with commune-, date-, and place-level detail; strongest subnational civilian numerator in the bundle.
limitations: Primarily Tutsi-victim counting; undetermined Hutu deaths were not registered; completeness of survivor/place/date fields varies across communes; does not provide local organized-armed death totals.
notes: Core source for the Kibuye episode and for the Bisesero auxiliary estimate.

title: The Death Toll of the Rwandan Genocide: A Detailed Analysis for Gikongoro Province
date_scope: 1990-2002 baseline and post-genocide reconstruction, used to infer 1994 losses
strengths: Uses local administrative population data by sector and a transparent comparison with the 1991 census; provides sex-differentiated Tutsi mortality estimates.
limitations: Absolute death levels are partly reconstructed from later sex-ratio and population structure rather than named-victim counts; not a combatant-loss source.
notes: Best current source cluster for a future Gikongoro episode, but still too model-dependent for a strong DR.

title: Contested Counting: Toward a Rigorous Estimate of the Death Toll in the Rwandan Genocide
strengths: Transparent national reconciliation of Tutsi deaths, with an explicit warning that multiple kinds of violence co-occurred and should not be mechanically merged.
limitations: National totals are not directly usable as subnational episode numerators or denominators.
notes: Used for context and for the source-disagreement discussion, not for Kibuye denominators.

title: Rwanda 1994: A Failure of Leadership and a Preventable Genocide
strengths: Useful synthesis on FAR and gendarmerie growth and on which elite FAR units led the initial Kigali killing.
limitations: Not a casualty registry and not an episode-specific force-exposure reconstruction for rural prefectures.

title: Leave None to Tell the Story: Genocide in Rwanda
author_or_org: Human Rights Watch / Alison Des Forges
pages_or_sections: Sections 'April 1994: The Month That Would Not End', 'The Organization', and 'The Rwandan Patriotic Front'
strengths: Rich witness-based narrative on perpetrator structure, civilian status, military roles, and the coexistence of genocide with civil-war fighting and RPF abuses.
limitations: Narrative and legal-document style; not a consistent numeric death series.
notes: Key source for actor classification and for explaining why national or mixed casualty totals should not be flattened into one DR numerator.

title: Clement Kayishema and Obed Ruzindana convicted of genocide
author_or_org: United Nations International Criminal Tribunal for Rwanda
strengths: Explicit legal findings on who attacked whom and when at major Kibuye massacre sites.
limitations: Does not provide a full province-wide death total; press release is less detailed than the full judgment.
notes: Primary side-attribution source for the Kibuye episode.

title: Casualty Estimates in the Rwandan Genocide
author_or_org: David A. Armstrong II, Christian Davenport, and Allan C. Stam
strengths: Useful demonstration that broad national casualty estimates vary dramatically depending on sources and assumptions.
limitations: Counts total casualties/violence, not a clean direct-deaths civilian-versus-military split; not episode-specific.
notes: Included mainly to explain why mixed national casualty averages are not used for the core DR path.

title: Bargaining and fighting in Rwanda and Burundi
strengths: Provides a concise national combatant death figure for direct fighting in the civil war.
limitations: National and war-wide; not usable as a local Kibuye or Gikongoro combatant numerator without violating subnational denominator rules.
notes: Retained only as a national ceiling/context figure.
